summary.intent: Fix a set of Autocomplete component bugs in the ars-ui repo (PR #685) based on Codex review findings, then push and get a clean review pass.
summary.outcome: Three Codex review threads from a prior pass were fixed and resolved in commit 7197ab4e; a second Codex pass produced three more findings (filter refresh after selection, IME composition suppression, Clear focus state) which were also fixed, tested, and committed with coverage kept above 93.87% on autocomplete/mod.rs.

repo: The project uses a custom `cargo xci --profile fast` gate with 10 steps including formatting, check, clippy, workspace unit tests, snapshots, and mutual-exclusion checks.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a GitHub issue comment on the PR, not through a dedicated UI.
The ars-ui repo lives at fogodev/ars-ui and uses Rust with cargo-llvm-cov for coverage reporting.
DOM Browser Tests rely on ChromeDriver and are prone to infra-level SIGKILL failures unrelated to code changes.
Coverage is tracked at the file level; autocomplete/mod.rs target is ~93.87% line coverage.

code.finding: Selection logic must refresh the visible filter after an uncontrolled input mutation to keep displayed items consistent with the newly selected label.
IME composition events must suppress highlight navigation keydown handling to avoid interfering with CJK/IME input.
Clear action must not synthesize a focused state when the input is currently unfocused; previously it incorrectly set focused state regardless of actual focus.
Home/End keys must only dispatch HighlightFirst/HighlightLast when the autocomplete popup is active; otherwise native caret movement should be preserved.
Controlled input prop sync must preserve selected_key when the synced text still matches the selected item label; only stale selections should clear.

workflow: Codex review threads can be resolved via the GitHub GraphQL API using the resolveReviewThread mutation with the thread ID.
GitHub does not allow rerunning a specific failed job while the parent workflow run is still active; must wait for the run to complete first.
cargo-llvm-cov requires a separate `cargo llvm-cov test` invocation followed by `cargo llvm-cov report`; the `--text` flag and `--output-path` are needed for file-level coverage detail.
New regression tests should be written to fail before applying fixes, verifying the test targets the right behavior.
Stale test assertions (e.g., expected visible item counts) can break after behavior fixes and need updating alongside the fix.

friction: The commit hook ran silently for an extended period with no output, requiring a manual wait before determining it succeeded.
GitHub blocked a job-level rerun (via /actions/jobs/{id}/rerun) because the parent workflow run was still active — the API returned an error rather than queuing the rerun.
DOM Browser Tests failed due to a ChromeDriver SIGKILL infra issue unrelated to the code change, adding noise to the CI signal.

cargo llvm-cov's coverage report CLI options (--show-missing-lines, --include-filename-regex) did not behave as expected, requiring a full-repo text dump and manual grep to isolate autocomplete coverage.
rustfmt formatting differences were caught only at the fast-gate stage, requiring an extra apply-patch/rerun cycle.
open_items: DOM Browser Tests ChromeDriver SIGKILL failure was identified as an infra flake but not root-caused or permanently resolved — needs a rerun once the workflow completes.

Two uncovered message branches for 1-result and plural-result live announcements were added as new tests in this session but their underlying production code paths should be verified for completeness.
A third Codex review pass was triggered at session end; findings from that pass (if any) are unresolved.
